name: implement-step0-data-quality
description: Implement Step 0 data quality validation: non-null invoice_id, positive open_amount, valid country, known legal entity. Return list of invalid invoices.
instruction:
In buying/service.py step_0_data_quality_validation:
1. Change from placeholder to real checks
2. Validate each invoice: invoice_id not null/empty, open_amount > 0, country matches expected, legal_entity_code not empty
3. Return step result with list of invalid invoices and reasons
4. If any invalid: status='warning' (continue with valid ones, not fail entire batch)
5. Add unit tests in test_buying_process.py
acceptance_criteria: Step 0 validates invoice_id, open_amount, country, legal_entity_code
Invalid invoices listed in result data
Unit tests cover valid and invalid cases

name: add-lock-release-to-buying-service
description: Buying service must explicitly release country lock after completion. Currently lock is only released by scheduler's finally block — if called directly (manual buy), lock stays until 30min TTL.
instruction: In buying/service.py run_buying_process: wrap steps in try/finally, call release_buy_lock in finally. Also in buying/router.py for auto-buy and run-process endpoints: ensure lock released on any exit path. Import release_buy_lock from scheduler.service.
acceptance_criteria: Lock released after run_buying_process regardless of success/failure
Unit test verifies lock release call

name: credit-limit-from-customers-master
description: Replace hardcoded 50K credit limit with per-customer values from customers_master Firestore collection.
instruction:
In buying/service.py step_4_credit_limit_check:
1. Load customers_master collection at start of step
2. Build lookup: {customer_id_str: credit_limit_float}
3. For each invoice: get credit_limit from lookup, fallback to DEFAULT_CREDIT_LIMIT if not found
4. Keep DEFAULT_CREDIT_LIMIT as fallback but log warning when used
5. Add unit test with mock customers_master data
acceptance_criteria: Credit limit read from customers_master collection
Fallback to DEFAULT_CREDIT_LIMIT with warning if customer not in master

name: buying-comprehensive-tests
description: Add comprehensive tests for buying process: step 0 validation, lock release, credit limit from master data, full pipeline edge cases.
instruction:
In tests/unit/:
1. test_buying_process.py: add tests for step_0 with valid/invalid invoices
2. test_buying_credit.py: add test with mock customers_master providing per-customer limits
3. test_scheduler_lock.py: verify lock release is called
4. Ensure all 8 eligibility rules have both positive and negative tests
5. Run full suite, verify 100% of buying logic has test coverage
acceptance_criteria: Step 0 tests cover valid and invalid invoice data
Credit limit tests use per-customer values

name: verify-o007
description: Deep-verify O-007: buying service improvements tested, docker build works, manual buy succeeds.
instruction:
1. python backend/tests/run_tests.py — all pass
2. docker compose up --build — containers start
3. Run manual buy from UI — verify Step 0 runs, Step 4 uses per-customer limits, lock released
4. Commit and push
